Title: Variation in a Poaceae-conserved fatty acid metabolic gene cluster controls rice yield by regulating male fertility

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: ABC transporter with ABC_ATP_DarD and ABC2_membrane domains
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: cutin deposition in anther surface/pollen exine, dehydration protection, male fertility, yield maintenance
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: anther surface; pollen exine; apoplast epidermis/cell wall
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Predominantly co-expressed in panicles; specific in anthers
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Predominantly co-expressed in panicles; specific in anthers
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Exports HMGs and hydroxy fatty acids to the apoplast; mutation reduces cutin deposition, impairs male fertility and lowers yield.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsABCG22 is a membrane-localized FGC3 transporter. 2. It significantly increases export of labeled HMG16:0 and C16:0(ω-OH) in Nicotiana protoplasts. 3. osabcg22 mutants lose anther/pollen lipid staining and show reduced HMG/cutin, male fertility and yield.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: subcellular localization; transport assay in Nicotiana benthamiana protoplasts using stable isotope substrates; CRISPR mutant phenotyping; histochemical staining; metabolite profiling
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Transporter target for improving cutin export or designing humidity-sensitive male sterility; may need combination with biosynthetic genes.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Mutation causes fertility defects and yield reduction
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: relative humidity affects fertility of low-HMG genotypes
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: More selective than OsLTPL82, with no clear export enhancement for C16:0.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: widely targeted metabolomics, OMIX006912; mGWAS; pGWAS

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Lipid transfer protein with AAI domain
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: cutin deposition in anther surface/pollen exine, dehydration protection, male fertility, yield maintenance
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: anther surface; pollen exine; apoplast epidermis/cell wall
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Predominantly co-expressed in panicles; specific in anthers
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Predominantly co-expressed in panicles; specific in anthers
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Broad-spectrum exporter of cutin monomers; mutation reduces lipid layer/cutin in anthers, impairs fertility and yield.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsLTPL82 is a plasma membrane-localized FGC3 transporter. 2. It significantly increases export of labeled HMG16:0 and C16:0(ω-OH), and also enhances C16:0 release. 3. osltpl82 mutants show reduced anther cutin/lipid staining, male fertility and yield.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: subcellular localization; stable isotope transport assays in Nicotiana protoplasts; CRISPR mutant phenotyping; histochemical staining; metabolite profiling
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising transporter target due to broader export activity; useful for engineering pollen cuticle integrity or HGMS systems.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Mutation causes fertility defects and yield reduction
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: relative humidity affects phenotypic expression of low-HMG states
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Compared with OsABCG22, OsLTPL82 has broader export activity toward cutin monomers; annotation was corrected in this study.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: widely targeted metabolomics, OMIX006912; mGWAS; pGWAS

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: cutin formation, protection of male gametophytes from dehydration, male fertility, yield maintenance
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: anther surface; pollen exine; apoplast epidermis/cell wall
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes HMG/cutin monomer accumulation; mutation reduces anther HMGs/cutin, causes defective anthers and pollen, lowers yield.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsFAR2 is a core FGC3 biosynthetic gene associated with fatty acid metabolites and yield. 2. Recombinant OsFAR2 converts C16:0-ACP, and less efficiently C16:0-CoA, to C16:0 alcohol in vitro. 3. osfar2 mutants show reduced HMG/cutin deposition in anthers and decreased male fertility and yield.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Predominantly co-expressed in panicles; GUS signal specific in anthers
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Predominantly co-expressed in panicles; specific in anthers
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces palmitoyl-ACP, and less efficiently palmitoyl-CoA, to hexadecanol (C16:0 alcohol).
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: in vitro enzyme assay with recombinant protein; subcellular localization; CRISPR mutant phenotyping; metabolite profiling; histochemical staining
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target to engineer HMG/cutin levels for humidity-sensitive male sterility and hybrid breeding; may improve fertility/yield under low humidity when combined with other FGC3 genes.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss of function causes male sterility/fertility defects and 30–62% yield reduction across FGC3 mutants; altered cuticle/lipid deposition
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: relative humidity affects fertility outcomes linked to HMG/cutin status
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Model proposes OsFAR2 acts in plastids before unidentified steps regenerate fatty acids that enter the ER; included in Poaceae-conserved FGC3.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: widely targeted metabolomics, OMIX006912; mGWAS; pGWAS

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: cutin formation, protection from dehydration, male fertility, yield maintenance
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: anther surface; pollen exine; apoplast epidermis/cell wall
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes HMG biosynthesis; mutation lowers anther HMG/cutin, impairs pollen development and reduces yield.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsKCS11 is a core FGC3 enzyme required for HMG production. 2. In vitro it forms C16:0-CoA from C16:0 and C16:0(ω-OH)-CoA from C16:0(ω-OH). 3. Together with OsCYP704B2 and OsGPAT6, it sequentially converts C16:0 or C18:0 substrates to HMGs. 4. oskcs11 mutants show reduced cutin monomers and male fertility.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Predominantly co-expressed in panicles; specific in anthers
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Predominantly co-expressed in panicles; specific in anthers
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control point in FGC3 sequence, but rate-limiting not established
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Activates fatty acids and hydroxy fatty acids by CoA ligation to generate acyl-CoA and hydroxyacyl-CoA intermediates used for HMG synthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: yeast microsome enzyme assay; combinatorial in vitro reconstitution; transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; CRISPR mutant phenotyping; metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for improving anther cuticle robustness or designing humidity-sensitive genic male sterility; may require stacking with OsCYP704B2 and OsGPAT6.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Mutation causes defective anthers/pollen and yield loss
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: relative humidity shapes fertility consequences of altered HMG levels
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Full-length transcript was corrected to contain both Os03g06700 and Os03g06705; orthologous enzymes from Poaceae retain same biochemical function.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: widely targeted metabolomics, OMIX006912; mGWAS; pGWAS

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: cutin formation, pollen/anther protection, male fertility, yield maintenance
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: anther surface; pollen exine; apoplast epidermis/cell wall
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Directly produces HMGs from hydroxyacyl-CoA and G3P; mutation lowers HMG/cutin and reduces fertility/yield.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsGPAT6 is the terminal acyltransferase of the FGC3 HMG biosynthetic route. 2. It uses C16:0-CoA and C16:0(ω-OH)-CoA with G3P to generate MG16:0 and HMG16:0. 3. With OsKCS11 and OsCYP704B2 it converts C16:0/C18:0 fatty acids to HMGs. 4. osgpat6 mutants have reduced anther cutin monomers and male fertility.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Predominantly co-expressed in panicles; specific in anthers
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Predominantly co-expressed in panicles; specific in anthers
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: terminal determinant within the defined HMG route, but rate-limiting not established
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers acyl or hydroxyacyl groups from acyl-CoA/hydroxyacyl-CoA to glycerol-3-phosphate to produce monoacylglycerol and hydroxymonoacylglycerol.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: yeast microsome enzyme assay; pathway reconstitution; transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; mutant phenotyping; metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong engineering target to elevate HMGs directly; can be combined with upstream FGC3 genes or regulatory MADS genes for hybrid breeding applications.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Mutation causes impaired male fertility and yield loss
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: relative humidity impacts fertility of low-HMG genotypes
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Original annotation was corrected; promoter polymorphisms correlate with stearate content and yield, and expression correlates with both traits.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: widely targeted metabolomics, OMIX006912; mGWAS; pGWAS

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: cutin formation, dehydration protection of pollen, male fertility, yield maintenance
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: anther surface; pollen exine; apoplast epidermis/cell wall
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Hydroxylates fatty acid substrates for HMG biosynthesis; mutation decreases cutin monomers and fertility/yield.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsCYP704B2 produces ω-hydroxylated fatty acid intermediates required for HMG synthesis. 2. It hydroxylates both C16:0 and C16:0-CoA to C16:0(ω-OH) and C16:0(ω-OH)-CoA. 3. With OsKCS11 and OsGPAT6 it reconstitutes HMG biosynthesis from C16:0/C18:0. 4. oscyp704b2 mutants show reduced anther HMG/cutin and male fertility.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Predominantly co-expressed in panicles; specific in anthers
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Predominantly co-expressed in panicles; specific in anthers
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: ω-hydroxylates palmitic acid and palmitoyl-CoA to generate hydroxy fatty acid/hydroxyacyl-CoA intermediates for HMG synthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: yeast microsome enzyme assay; reconstitution with OsKCS11 and OsGPAT6; transient expression; mutant phenotyping; metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for engineering cutin monomer levels and humidity-sensitive male sterility; can be stacked with OsKCS11 and OsGPAT6.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Mutation impairs male fertility and reduces yield
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: relative humidity modifies fertility phenotype of low-HMG backgrounds
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Promoter polymorphisms and expression are associated with stearate content and yield; orthologous proteins in Poaceae retain function.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: widely targeted metabolomics, OMIX006912; mGWAS; pGWAS

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: anther cuticle formation, pollen dehydration protection, male fertility, yield maintenance
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positive regulator of FGC3-dependent hydroxylated cutin monomer accumulation; mutation causes severe male sterility and dramatic yield loss.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsMADS6 directly regulates FGC3. 2. It activates promoters of FGC3 synthesis genes and significantly activates OsLTPL82 among transport genes. 3. It binds FGC3 promoters in vivo. 4. osmads6 mutants reduce expression of OsKCS11, OsCYP704B2, OsGPAT6 and OsLTPL82 and show ~98–99% yield loss. 5. Transient expression increases HFAs and HMGs.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Major positive developmental regulator linking floral MADS control to cutin monomer biosynthesis/transport and male fertility.
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: biosynthesis and transport of hydroxylated cutin monomers
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates OsKCS11, OsCYP704B2, OsGPAT6, OsFAR2 promoter activity in dual-LUC; significantly activates OsLTPL82 among transport genes; mutant reduces OsKCS11, OsCYP704B2, OsGPAT6 and OsLTPL82 expression
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: relative humidity influences phenotypic consequence of reduced HMG accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: co-expression analysis; dual-luciferase assay; ChIP-qPCR; mutant analysis; transient expression and metabolite profiling
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential master regulator for tuning humidity-sensitive genic male sterility and hybrid breeding, but severe sterility of loss-of-function lines requires careful deployment.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss of function causes near-complete male sterility and 98–99% yield reduction
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: T-DNA insertion mutant used; unlike OsMADS17, its effect on transport genes appears selective, strongest for OsLTPL82.
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: widely targeted metabolomics, OMIX006912; co-expression analysis

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: anther cuticle formation, pollen dehydration protection, male fertility, yield maintenance
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positive regulator of FGC3 expression and cutin monomer accumulation; mutation decreases FGC3 expression, male fertility and yield.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsMADS17 directly regulates FGC3. 2. It activates promoters of FGC3 synthesis members. 3. It binds all FGC3 member promoters in vivo and binds the OsKCS11 promoter in yeast one-hybrid. 4. osmads17 mutants show reduced expression of all FGC3 members. 5. Transient expression increases HFAs and HMGs.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Positive regulator coordinating expression of the entire FGC3 cluster to sustain hydroxylated cutin monomer production and male fertility.
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: biosynthesis and transport of hydroxylated cutin monomers
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates FGC3 synthesis gene promoters; mutant significantly decreases expression of all FGC3 members
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: OsFAR2; OsKCS11; OsGPAT6; OsCYP704B2; OsABCG22; OsLTPL82
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: relative humidity influences outcome of reduced HMG levels
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: co-expression analysis; dual-luciferase assay; ChIP-qPCR; yeast one-hybrid; CRISPR mutant analysis; transient expression/metabolite profiling
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful regulator target for coordinated manipulation of FGC3 and HGMS-type breeding systems.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss of function reduces FGC3 expression, male fertility and yield
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts more broadly than OsMADS6 at transcript level, reducing all FGC3 members in mutant plants.
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: widely targeted metabolomics, OMIX006912; co-expression analysis
